title: eriophorum gracile subsp. asiaticum
intro: Eriophorum gracile subsp. asiaticum, also known as the Asian cotton grass, is a subspecies of the Eriophorum gracile plant, which is a type of flowering plant in the sedge family (Cyperaceae). It is native to East Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Eriophorum gracile subsp. asiaticum include reducing fever, treating respiratory infections, and alleviating symptoms of bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the roots and rhizomes of the plant are used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
